info.title: Vanta API
info.description: The Vanta REST API lets customers query and mutate Vanta's data. Use this API to automate bulk actions, query data for custom workflows and dashboards, and bolster your security operations.

fileName.description: The file name of the document.

uploadedBy.description: The actor who uploaded this document. It could be a user or an app.

creationDate.description: Date of when the document was uploaded.

updatedDate.description: Date when the document was last updated.

deletionDate.description: Date of when the document was deleted. Is set to null if the document has not been deleted.

organizationId.description: The unique identifier for the organization.

email.description: The email address of the auditor.

givenName.description: The given name (first name) of the auditor.

familyName.description: The family name (last name) of the auditor.

givenName.description: First name of the new user.

familyName.description: Last name of the new user.

id.description: The unique identifier for the comment

auditEvidenceId.description: The unique identifier for the audit evidence related to the comment.

creationDate.description: When the comment was created

email.description: The email of the comment author. This acts as a unique identifier to map users between Vanta and external systems.

authorName.description:
Human-readable display name of the comment author.
Null if the author's name is not available (e.g., user was deleted).
This enables correct author attribution in integrations where users cannot
be reliably matched across systems by email alone.

email.description: Email of author. Must match an existing Vanta user and the user must exist under the Audit Firm who is making the API request

creationDate.description: When the comment was created in the external system

displayName.description: Name of the person that is shown in product.

domains.description: The security domains that the control belongs to.

owner.description: The control's owner.

role.description: The control's GDPR role, if the control is a GDPR control.

customFields.description: The control's custom field values, if control custom fields is included in your Vanta instance.

creationDate.description: When the control was created. Returns null for Vanta library controls.

modificationDate.description: When the control was last modified. Returns null for Vanta library controls.

externalId.description: The external id of the control.

effectiveDate.description: The effective date of the control.

category.description: The category of the control. See the ControlDomain enum for possible values.

sections.description: Framework sections that the control should be mapped to.

role.description:
The GDPR role of the control, which specifies whether the data is being "collected" or "processed". See the GdprRole enum for possible values.
This field should only be included for controls that are to be mapped to the GDPR framework.

InformationRequestApprovalStatus.description:
Current approval status of the information request, tracking its lifecycle through the audit process.

The status progresses through the workflow: initial state → awaiting review → approved or flagged.
Status can move between awaiting review and flagged states as evidence is reviewed and resubmitted.

InformationRequestCadence.description: Frequency cadence for the information request, indicating how often it recurs.

InformationRequestType.description:
Type of information request, defining what scope of evidence is needed.

- POINT_IN_TIME: Evidence for a specific moment (e.g., current state of a policy)
- POPULATION: Evidence covering all items in a category (e.g., all employees)
- SAMPLE: Evidence for a representative sample (e.g., 10 random customer records)

ResourceOwner.description: Represents either a user or a team owning a resource.

InformationRequest.description:
Information Request resource representing a single request for audit evidence from a customer.

An information request is created by an auditor and shared with the customer organization.
The customer then uploads evidence, which the auditor reviews and either approves or flags
for issues.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the information request within Vanta's system.
This is the primary identifier used in all API endpoints.
Format: ObjectId as a string (e.g., "6890e473dce1da5d8406f5e7")

uniqueId.description:
External unique ID to prevent duplicates across different audit systems.
Used for idempotency when syncing data between external audit management
systems and Vanta. Unlike `id`, this value is provided by the external system.

additionalControlIds.description:
Additional control IDs beyond those automatically mapped from framework codes.
Allows manual association with specific controls when automatic mapping
is insufficient. Each ID should reference a valid control in your audit framework.

approvalStatus.description:
Current approval status tracking the request's lifecycle through evidence
submission and auditor review.

cadence.description:
How frequently this information request recurs (e.g., annual password
policy reviews). Null for one-time requests.

frameworkCodes.description:
The framework codes this request addresses.
Links the request to specific compliance requirements. Can be an empty array
if no framework codes are associated. These codes correspond to standards like SOC 2, ISO 27001, etc.

description.description:
Detailed description explaining what evidence is needed and why.
Should provide clear instructions to help the customer understand what
to submit.

dueDate.description:
The deadline by which the customer must fulfill this request.
Null if no specific deadline is set. Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

evidenceCaptureDate.description:
The earliest date for which evidence should be captured.
Evidence dated before this date may not be accepted.
Null if not restricted. Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

requestId.description:
Non-unique external reference ID for this request.
Unlike `uniqueId` which must be unique, `requestId` is for display/reference
purposes only (e.g., "REQ-123"). Null if not provided.

requestType.description: Defines the scope of evidence required.

title.description: Short, descriptive title summarizing what is being requested.

creationDate.description:
Timestamp when the request was created in the system.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

modificationDate.description:
Timestamp when the request was last modified.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

deletionDate.description:
Timestamp when the request was soft-deleted. Null if the request has not
been deleted. Soft deletes allow retaining history while hiding the request
from normal operations.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

ownerAssignment.description:
Resource owner (user or team) assigned to this information request.
Returns null if no resource owner is assigned.

AcceptInformationRequestEvidenceInput.description:
Input for accepting evidence on an information request, indicating that
the submitted evidence meets requirements. This changes the request's
approvalStatus to an approved state and creates an activity log entry.

auditorEmail.description:
Email of the auditor accepting the evidence.
Must match an existing Vanta user who belongs to the audit firm making the API request.

InformationRequestComment.description:
A comment on an information request enables communication between auditors
and customers regarding evidence requirements and submissions.

These threaded discussions help clarify requests, explain or resolve questions about evidence,
and are always visible to both parties once created.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the comment within Vanta's system.
Format: ObjectId as a string (e.g., "6890e473dce1da5d8406f5e7").

text.description:
The comment message content.
Can include explanations, questions, or clarifications about the information request.

creationDate.description:
Timestamp when the comment was created.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

modificationDate.description:
Timestamp when the comment was last edited.
Null if the comment has never been modified.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

deletionDate.description:
Timestamp when the comment was soft-deleted.
Null if the comment has not been deleted.
Soft deletes retain the comment for audit history while hiding it from normal operations.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

email.description:
Email address of the comment author.
This email uniquely identifies users between Vanta and external audit systems.

authorName.description:
Human-readable display name of the comment author.
Null if the author's name is not available (e.g., user was deleted).
This enables correct author attribution in integrations where users cannot
be reliably matched across systems by email alone.

AddInformationRequestCommentInput.description:
Comments enable communication between auditors and customers about
evidence requirements and submissions. All comments are immediately
visible to both parties once created.

text.description:
The text content of the comment.
Must be at least 1 character. Can include questions, clarifications,
or explanations related to the information request.

email.description:
Email address of the comment author.
Must match an existing Vanta user who belongs to the audit firm making the API request.
This email uniquely identifies the author across systems.

creationDate.description:
Timestamp when the comment was created in the external audit management system.
This allows synchronizing comment timestamps from external systems.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

id.description: Internal id of the custom evidence request within Vanta

controlIds.description: A set of controls, referenced by id, to map the evidence to

reminderWindow.description: Duration representing when to send notifications, relative to renewal date

isRestricted.description: Whether this document contains sensitive data and needs more restrictive read access

controlIds.description: A set of controls, referenced by id, to map the evidence to

reminderWindow.description: Duration representing when to send notifications, relative to renewal date

isRestricted.description: Whether this document contains sensitive data and needs more restrictive read access

auditorEmail.description: Email of the auditor who created the custom evidence request.

DeleteInformationRequestCommentInput.description:
Deletes an existing comment on an information request.
Only the original author of the comment can delete it.

email.description:
Email address of the comment author.
Must match an existing Vanta user who belongs to the audit firm making the API request.
This email uniquely identifies the author across systems.

CreateInformationRequestInput.description:
Input for creating a new information request.
New requests are created in an initial state by default.

uniqueId.description:
External unique ID to prevent duplicate requests across audit systems.
Must be unique within the audit. Used for idempotency.

title.description:
Short, descriptive title summarizing what is being requested.
Must be at least 1 character.

description.description:
Detailed description explaining what evidence is needed.
Helps the customer understand what to submit. Optional.

frameworkCodes.description:
Framework codes this request addresses.
An empty array if no framework codes are associated.

cadence.description: How frequently this request recurs. Null or omitted for one-time requests.

dueDate.description:
Deadline for fulfilling this request. Null or omitted if no deadline.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

evidenceCaptureDate.description:
Start date of the audit period. Evidence before this date may not be accepted.
Null or omitted if not restricted.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

id.description: The unique identifier for the audit.

customerOrganizationName.description: The domain name of the customer organization being audited (e.g. vanta.com)

customerDisplayName.description: The human readable name of the customer organization being audited (e.g. Vanta)

customerOrganizationId.description: The uuid of the customer organization being audited

auditStartDate.description: The start of the audit window. This is also when data collection for audit starts.

auditEndDate.description: The end of the audit window.

earlyAccessStartsAt.description: Timestamp at which auditors gain access to the audit. Occurs before the audit window begins

framework.description: The name of the framework for the audit

displayName.description:
The display name for the audit. Returns the custom audit name if set,
otherwise returns the framework name.

allowAuditorEmails.description: Emails of auditors with access to audit

allowAllAuditors.description: Set to true if all auditors in audit firm have access

deletionDate.description: Timestamp when the audit was deleted

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the audit was created

modificationDate.description: Timestamp when the audit was updated

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the audit was marked completed, and report was uploaded

auditFocus.description: Audit focus determines if the audit is internal or external facing

requestsSharedWithCustomer.description: Timestamp when information requests were shared with the customer. Null if not shared.

auditorRequestListMetadata.description:
Metadata about the auditor request list. This field is only present for IRL (Information
Request List) based audits and will be undefined for standard audits. Use the presence
of this field to differentiate between IRL and non-IRL audits.

FlagInformationRequestEvidenceInput.description:
Input for flagging evidence on an information request, indicating issues
that need to be addressed. This changes the request's approvalStatus to
a flagged state and creates an activity log entry.

The customer will be notified and can resubmit evidence after addressing the issues.

auditorEmail.description:
Email of the auditor flagging the evidence.
Must match an existing Vanta user who belongs to the audit firm making the API request.

reason.description:
Detailed explanation of what issues were found with the evidence.
This reason is visible to the customer and guides them on what to fix.
Must be at least 1 character.

frameworkCodes.description:
Array of valid framework codes for the audit's framework (e.g., "CC6.1", "CC6.2").
These represent the different framework sections available for creating information requests.

PageInfo.description: Provides information about the pagination of a dataset.

endCursor.description: The cursor that points to the end of the current page, or null if there is no such cursor.

hasNextPage.description: Indicates if there is another page after the current page.

hasPreviousPage.description: Indicates if there is a page before the current page.

startCursor.description: The cursor that points to the start of the current page, or null if there is no such cursor.

PageCursor.description:
A marker or pointer, telling the API where to start fetching items for the subsequent page in a paginated dataset.
Note that the requested page will not include the item that corresponds to this cursor but will start from the one immediately
after this cursor.

PageSize.description: Controls the maximum number of items returned in one response from the API.

VulnerableAssetType.description: VulnerableAssetType describes the types of assets a vulnerability is on.

value.description: Value of key-value pair.

resourceId.description: The scanned asset's Vanta resource id.

integrationId.description: Integration that the the vulnerable asset is scanned by.

imageDigest.description: Digest of the scanned container image.

imagePushedAtDate.description: Push date of the scanned container image.

imageTags.description: Tags of the scanned container image.

parentAccountOrOrganization.description: The parent account or organization of the scanned asset.

biosUuid.description: BIOS UUID of the scanned asset.

macAddresses.description: Mac addresses of the scanned asset.

hostnames.description: Host names of the scanned asset.

operatingSystems.description: Operating systems of the scanned asset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the vulnerable asset.

name.description: Display name of the vulnerable asset.

assetType.description:
Type of the vulnerable asset.
Possible values: CODE_REPOSITORY, CONTAINER_REPOSITORY, CONTAINER_REPOSITORY_IMAGE, MANIFEST_FILE, SERVER, SERVERLESS_FUNCTION, WORKSTATION.

hasBeenScanned.description: Whether the vulnerable asset has been scanned.

imageScanTag.description: Only relevant for container repositories. This field sets the container image tag that vulnerabilities will be retrieved for. If null, the latest image will be retrieved.

scanners.description: The integrations that are scanning this vulnerable asset.

principle.description: The principle that groups this section, if any.

domains.description: The security domains that the control belongs to.

owner.description: The control's owner.

role.description: The control's GDPR role, if the control is a GDPR control.

customFields.description: The control's custom field values, if control custom fields is included in your Vanta instance.

creationDate.description: When the control was created. Returns null for Vanta library controls.

modificationDate.description: When the control was last modified. Returns null for Vanta library controls.

framework.description: The report standard framework fulfilled by the control.

sections.description: Sections of a framework that this control satisfies

name.description: Name of control associated to this evidence

sectionNames.description: A list sections associated to the control

externalId.description: This is a static UUID to map Audit Firm controls to Vanta controls

status.description: Vanta internal statuses for audit evidence

name.description: Mutable name for evidence. Not guaranteed to be unique.

deletionDate.description: The date this Audit Evidence was deleted

creationDate.description: The date this Audit Evidence was created

statusUpdatedDate.description: Point in time that status was last updated

testStatus.description: The outcome of the automated test run, for Test-type evidence

relatedControls.description: The controls associated to this evidence

description.description: The description for the evidence. It will be set to null if the evidence is deleted

isDownloadable.description: Set to true if this is a presigned s3 url. Set to false if this is a customer uploaded link

mimeType.description: MIME type of the evidence file (e.g., "application/pdf", "image/png")

ListAuditsPageSize.description:
Controls the maximum number of items returned in one response from the listAudits API.

We have increased the pagination size for this endpoint to enable our upmarket audit firms to
sync in a timely manner. The rest of the API will use a pagination size of 100.

InformationRequestActivityLog.description:
Activity log entry tracking changes and actions on an information request.

The activity log provides a complete audit trail of all operations performed
on an information request, including status changes, evidence uploads, edits,
and evidence sharing. This enables tracking compliance activities and understanding
the request's history.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the activity log entry within Vanta's system.
Format: ObjectId as a string (e.g., "6890e473dce1da5d8406f5e7").

activityType.description:
Type of activity that occurred on the information request.
Determines which additional fields are populated.

timestamp.description:
Timestamp when the activity occurred.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

userEmail.description:
Email address of the user who performed the activity.
Null for system-generated activities (rare, such as automated status changes).
This email uniquely identifies users across systems.

oldStatus.description:
Previous approval status before the status change.
Only populated for status change activities. Null for all other activity types.

newStatus.description:
New approval status after the status change.
Only populated for status change activities. Null for all other activity types.

reason.description:
Optional explanation for the status change.
Only populated for status change activities when a reason is provided
(e.g., when flagging evidence). Null for all other cases.

ComputerStatusOutcome.description:
The possible outcomes of a status check. The outcome can be one of the following:
FAIL:  The check is failing.
IN_PROGRESS: The check needs further data from the given computer in order to evaluate. The field(s) needed from a computer to calculate the ComputerStatusOutcome were null.
NA: The check is not applicable for the given computer.
PASS: The check is passing.

ComputerStatus.description: The a status check for a computer. Representation for screenlock, diskEncryption, passwordManager, and antivirusInstallation.

outcome.description: The outcome of the status check.

OperatingSystemType.description: The possible types of the operating system. One of `mac_OS`, `linux`, or `windows`.

OperatingSystem.description: The computer's operating system type and version.

type.description: The type of the operating system.

version.description: The version of the operating system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the monitored computer.

integrationId.description: Hard-coded enums for Vanta-built integrations or application IDs for 3rd-party-built integrations.

lastCheckDate.description: Date of the computer's most recent report.

screenlock.description: Whether or not the computer has screenlock enabled.

diskEncryption.description: Whether or not the computer's hard drive is encrypted.

passwordManager.description: Whether or not the computer has a password manager installed.

antivirusInstallation.description: Whether or not the computer has antivirus software installed.

operatingSystem.description: The computer's operating system name and version.

owner.description: The name, unique identifier, and email address of the computer's owner.

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the computer. This value may be null if it is not reported by the device.

udid.description: The universal device id of the computer.

ComputerStatusFilter.description:
Enum representing computer compliance statuses that can be utilized as a filter. The meanings are as follows:
AV_NOT_INSTALLED: The computer does not have antivirus software installed.
HD_NOT_ENCRYPTED: The computer's harddrive is not encrypted.
LAST_CHECK_OVER_14_DAYS: No data has been received from computer for over 14 days.
PWM_NOT_INSTALLED: The computer does not have a password manager installed.
SCREENLOCK_NOT_CONFIGURED: The computer does not have screenlock configured appropriately.

UploadedDocumentEvidence.description:
Evidence in the form of an uploaded document file.
The URL is a temporary presigned URL that expires after a certain period.

id.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded document in Vanta's system.

filename.description:
The original filename of the uploaded document.
Undefined if the filename was not captured during upload.

mimeType.description:
The MIME type of the document indicating its file format.
Common types: "application/pdf", "image/png", "image/jpeg", "text/plain"

url.description:
A presigned URL for downloading the document.
This URL is temporary and expires after a limited time.
Request a new evidence list to get a fresh URL if expired.

LinkEvidence.description: Evidence in the form of a link to external documentation or resources.

title.description: The title or descriptive name for the link.

description.description: Optional description providing additional context about the link.

url.description: The URL pointing to the external documentation or resource.

ObservationEvidence.description: Evidence in the form of a text observation or note.

observationDetails.description: The text content of the observation.

VantaDocumentUploadedEvidence.description:
Represents an uploaded file in a Vanta Document.
The file is stored in Vanta's system and accessed via a presigned URL.

type.description: Discriminator to identify this as an uploaded file.

id.description: The unique identifier for the file version in Vanta's system.

filename.description:
The original filename of the uploaded document.
Undefined if the filename was not captured during upload.

mimeType.description:
The MIME type of the document indicating its file format.
Common types: "application/pdf", "image/png", "image/jpeg", "text/plain"

url.description:
A presigned URL for downloading the document.
This URL is temporary and expires after a limited time.
Request a new evidence list to get a fresh URL if expired.

VantaDocumentLinkEvidence.description:
Represents an external link in a Vanta Document.
The link points to documentation hosted outside of Vanta's system.

type.description: Discriminator to identify this as an external link.

id.description: The unique identifier for the file version in Vanta's system.

url.description: The URL pointing to the external documentation or resource.

VantaDocumentEvidence.description:
Evidence in the form of a Vanta Document.
Vanta Documents are managed documents with metadata like expiration dates
and versioning. They can either be uploaded files stored in Vanta or
links to external documentation.

title.description: The title of the document as displayed in Vanta.

description.description: Optional description providing additional context about the document.

expirationDate.description:
The date when the document version expires and should be renewed.
Undefined if no expiration is set.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

addedBy.description:
Information about the user who added this file version to the document.
Undefined if the user information is not available.

file.description:
The file or link associated with this document version.
Use the `type` discriminator to determine whether it's an uploaded file
or an external link.

VantaPolicyEvidence.description:
Evidence in the form of a Vanta Policy.
Vanta Policies are compliance policies with metadata like locale,
effective dates, and versioning. Policies are always uploaded files
stored in Vanta's system.

title.description: The title of the policy as displayed in Vanta.

description.description: Optional description providing additional context about the policy.

expirationDate.description:
The date when the policy version expires and should be renewed.
Undefined if no expiration is set.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

lastEditedBy.description:
Information about the user who last edited this policy version.
Undefined if the user information is not available.

policyId.description:
The policy type this evidence belongs to (e.g. "information-security-policy-bsi").
Policies prefixed with "custom-" are customer-defined; others are Vanta-provided templates.

policyVersionId.description:
The identifier of the approved policy version that was submitted as evidence.
A new version is created each time a policy is updated and approved.

policyVersionFileId.description:
The identifier of the locale-specific file within the policy version.
Each policy version may contain multiple files, one per supported language.

effectiveOrCreationDate.description:
The date when this policy version became effective, or when it was created.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

locale.description:
The locale/language of the policy document.
Examples: "EN" for English, "ES" for Spanish, "FR" for French

url.description:
A presigned URL for downloading the policy document.
This URL is temporary and expires after a limited time.
Request a new evidence list to get a fresh URL if expired.

mimeType.description:
The MIME type of the policy document indicating its file format.
Common types: "application/pdf", "application/msword"

filename.description:
The original filename of the uploaded policy document.
Undefined if the filename was not captured during upload.

id.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded policy file in Vanta's system.

file.description: The uploaded policy file for this version.

VantaTestEvidence.description:
Evidence in the form of a Vanta automated test run.
Vanta Tests are automated compliance tests with metadata like test status,
execution time, and file type. Tests generate evidence files that can be
workpapers, raw data, or API request logs.

title.description: The title of the test run as displayed in Vanta.

testId.description: The unique identifier for the test in Vanta's system.

snapshotId.description:
The unique identifier for the point-in-time capture of this test run's evidence.
A snapshot is an immutable record of all files (workpapers, raw data, etc.)
generated during a single test execution.

testRunStatus.description: The outcome status of the test run.

executedAt.description:
The date and time when the test was executed.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

fileType.description: The type of evidence file included in this evidence.

mimeType.description:
The MIME type of the evidence file.
Common types: "application/pdf" for workpapers, "text/csv" for raw data

url.description:
A presigned URL for downloading the evidence file.
This URL is temporary and expires after a limited time.
Request a new evidence list to get a fresh URL if expired.

file.description: The evidence file for this test run.

InformationRequestEvidenceUnion.description:
Union type representing the different forms of evidence content.
The specific type is determined by the `evidenceType` field in InformationRequestEvidence.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the evidence within Vanta's system.
Format: ObjectId as a string (e.g., "6890e473dce1da5d8406f5e7").

evidenceType.description:
Type of evidence attached to this information request.
Determines the structure of the `evidence` field.

visibleToAuditorDate.description:
Timestamp when the evidence was made visible to the auditor.
Null if the customer has not yet shared this evidence with the auditor.
Evidence must be shared before the auditor can review it.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

creationDate.description:
Timestamp when the evidence was created/uploaded.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

modificationDate.description:
Timestamp when the evidence was last modified.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

deletionDate.description:
Timestamp when the evidence was soft-deleted.
Null if the evidence has not been deleted.
Soft deletes retain evidence in the system for audit history while hiding
it from normal operations.
Format: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.

evidence.description: The evidence content. Structure varies based on evidenceType.

ExternalFindingSeverity.description: ExternalFindingSeverity describes the severity of an external finding (Vulnerability or Security Alert)

integrationId.description: Integration that the vulnerability is scanned by.

packageIdentifier.description:
Identifier for the package that the vulnerability is found on.
Only relevant to vulnerabilities of type COMMON or GROUPED.

vulnerabilityType.description:
Type of the vulnerability.
Possible values: CONFIGURATION, COMMON, GROUPED.

targetId.description: Unique identifier for the underlying resource that the vulnerability is found on.

firstDetectedDate.description: Date when the vulnerability was first detected by Vanta.

sourceDetectedDate.description: Date when the vulnerability was first detected by the source.

lastDetectedDate.description: Date when the vulnerability was last detected.

severity.description:
Severity of the vulnerability.
Possible values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL.

cvssSeverityScore.description: CVSS severity score of the vulnerability.

remediateByDate.description: Date when the vulnerability should be remediated by.

relatedVulns.description:
Related vulnerabilities.
Only relevant to vulnerabilities of type GROUPED.

scanSource.description: Scanning tool that detected the vulnerability

isVulnDeactivatedIndefinitely.description: Whether the vulnerability is deactivated indefinitely.

deactivatedUntilDate.description: Date when the vulnerability will be reactivated.

deactivatedOnDate.description: Date when the vulnerability was deactivated.

deactivatedBy.description: Identifier of the user who deactivated the vulnerability.

deactivateMetadata.description: Metadata for the deactivation of the vulnerability.

EmploymentStatus.description:
The employment status of a person:
- UPCOMING: The person is not yet employed and will start employment in the future.
- CURRENT: The person is currently employed.
- ON_LEAVE: The person is on leave.
- INACTIVE: The person's employment is inactive.
- FORMER: The person was previously employed.

LeaveStatus.description: User can be active or upcoming leave period

startDate.description: The start of the person's leave.

endDate.description: The end of the person's leave. Null endDate implies indefinite leave.

status.description: ACTIVE if the leave is currently ongoing. UPCOMING if the startDate is in the future.

VantaBasedPersonInfoSource.description: The person's information comes from what is set in Vanta.

ScimBasedPersonInfoSource.description: The person's information comes from SCIM.

IntegrationBasedPersonInfoSource.description: The person's information comes from an integration.

PersonInfoSource.description: The source of the person's information.

TasksSummaryStatus.description:
The overall status of a person's outstanding tasks:
- NONE: There are no tasks.
- DUE_SOON: At least one task is due soon.
- OVERDUE: At least one task is overdue. Has a higher priority than DUE_SOON.
- COMPLETE: All tasks are complete.
- PAUSED: All tasks are paused.
- OFFBOARDING_DUE_SOON: At least one offboarding task is due soon.
- OFFBOARDING_OVERDUE: At least one offboarding task is overdue. Has a higher priority than OFFBOARDING_DUE_SOON.
- OFFBOARDING_COMPLETE: All offboarding tasks are complete.

TaskType.description:
The type a task summary falls into.
COMPLETE_TRAININGS: The task summary containing security trainings.
ACCEPT_POLICIES: The task summary containing policy acceptance.
COMPLETE_CUSTOM_TASKS: The task summary containing custom tasks.
INSTALL_DEVICE_MONITORING: The task summary containing device monitoring installation.

CustomerTrustExportStatusResponse.description:
Detailed status and result of a questionnaire export.
When `status` is `"completed"`, the response includes a time-limited download URL.

id.description: Unique identifier for the export job.

status.description:
Processing status of the export.
- `"pending"`: Export is still being processed.
- `"completed"`: Export finished successfully. Download URL is available.
- `"failed"`: Export failed. See `errorMessage` for details.

format.description: The output format of the exported file.

requestedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the export was requested.

completedAt.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the export completed successfully.
Only present when `status` is `"completed"`.

downloadUrl.description:
Pre-signed URL for downloading the exported file. Valid for 24 hours from the time of this response.
Only present when `status` is `"completed"`.

expiresAt.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the download URL expires. After this time, request a new export.
Only present when `status` is `"completed"`.

failedAt.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the export failed.
Only present when `status` is `"failed"`.

errorMessage.description:
Human-readable description of why the export failed.
Only present when `status` is `"failed"`.

Monthly: P1M - The document needs to be renewed monthly.

Annually: P1Y - The document needs to be renewed annually.

Day: P1D - A reminder will be sent one day before the renewal date.
Week: P1W - A reminder will be sent one week before the renewal date.
Month: P1M - A reminder will be sent one month before the renewal date.
Quarter: P3M - A reminder will be sent one quarter before the renewal date.

description.description:
This describes an actual or potential risk to your organization's people, processes, technology, data, and facilities.
Document actual issues or likely scenarios based on your specific environment or a potential vulnerability.

isSensitive.description: If set to true this risk can only be seen by its owner or users with Admin, RiskSensitiveManage or RiskSensitiveView permissions.

likelihood.description:
Represents the probability of an incident occurring due to this risk or vulnerability, expressed as a numerical score.
Defaults to a range of 1-5, where higher values indicate greater likelihood. The range can be customized in the Risk Management settings.
A value of `null` indicates that no score has been assigned.

impact.description:
Represents the potential severity of harm to your organization’s operations if this risk is exploited, expressed as a numerical score.
Defaults to a range of 1-5, where higher values indicate greater impact. The range can be customized in the Risk Management settings.
A value of `null` indicates that no score has been assigned.

residualLikelihood.description:
Represents the adjusted probability of this risk being exploited or affecting operations after implementing risk treatments, such as controls or mitigations.
Expressed as a numerical score, defaulting to a range of 1-5. The range can be customized in the Risk Management settings.
A value of `null` indicates that no score has been assigned.

residualImpact.description:
Represents the adjusted severity of harm to your organization’s operations if this risk is exploited after implementing risk treatments, such as controls or mitigations.
Expressed as a numerical score, defaulting to a range of 1-5. The range can be customized in the Risk Management settings.
A value of `null` indicates that no score has been assigned.

categories.description: The list of categories this risk scenario belongs to.

ciaCategories.description:
A list of the following for the type of risk documented:
- Confidentiality: Risk to data stores, customer/sensitive information, etc.
- Integrity: Risk to accuracy or integrity of system settings and/or data
- Availability: Risk to normal service operations and critical system functionality

treatment.description:
Indicate how your leadership team wants to address an identified risk. Please note: not all risks need to be addressed immediately (or at all). Your Risk Treatment decision will depend on multiple factors, such as your organization's risk tolerance and the value of the asset that the risk is associated with. The options are:
- Mitigate: Identify controls to put in place or tasks to be done that will reduce the risk score.
- Transfer: Move risk outside of your organization's set of responsibilities e.g. get cyber liability insurance.
- Avoid: Stop doing the activity which is causing the risk to your organization and its assets.
- Accept: Decide to live with the risk and take no further actions. - Accept: decide to live with the risk; this may be because it is highly unlikely, has a low financial or operational impact, or the cost and effort to treat the risk far exceeds the value of the asset

put.description:
To send us data regarding employee/contractor background checks, you send us `BackgroundCheckConnector` resources. This helps us determine current background check statuses.

This call replaces ALL existing `BackgroundCheckConnector` resources for the given app and `source_id` - this is a "state of the world" sync. In other words, if a `BackgroundCheckConnector` resource is sent for a user in a previous `sync_all` call, but is not included in a later `sync_all` call for that customer, it is assumed to no longer exist and is deleted in Vanta.

** Headers **
```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

** Scopes **
```
connectors.self:write-resource
```

** Schema **

Expand the `resources` array below for the schema of `BackgroundCheckConnector`.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Vanta generated identifier for the given resource, and can be found on the developer console page. See the list of registered resources and their IDs on https://app.vanta.com/settings/developer-console/<app_id>?tab=resources

post.description:
Accepts evidence for an information request, confirming that all submitted evidence
meets audit requirements. This action changes the request's approvalStatus to
an approved state and creates an activity log entry.

Acceptance workflow:
1. Auditor reviews submitted evidence
2. If evidence is satisfactory, auditor calls this endpoint
3. Request status changes to approved state and is considered complete for this audit cycle

Use this endpoint when:
- All required evidence has been submitted
- Evidence quality meets audit standards
- Evidence addresses all specified framework codes
- No additional information is needed
post.summary: Accept evidence for an information request

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of comments for an information request, enabling
auditors to view communication history and collaborate with customers.

This endpoint always includes soft-deleted records (where `deletionDate !== null`).
Clients should check the `deletionDate` field to identify and handle deleted records
appropriately in their systems.

This endpoint supports delta synchronization via the `changedSinceDate` parameter,
allowing efficient polling for changes without retrieving the entire dataset.

Pagination usage:
1. Make initial request with desired `pageSize`
2. Check `results.pageInfo.hasNextPage` to see if more data exists
3. If true, use `results.pageInfo.endCursor` as `pageCursor` in next request
4. Repeat until `hasNextPage` is false

Delta sync usage:
1. Store the timestamp of your last sync
2. Pass that timestamp as `changedSinceDate`
3. Only comments created, modified, or deleted since that timestamp are returned
4. Process updates, including soft-deletes (deletionDate !== null)
5. Update your last sync timestamp to the current time
get.summary: List comments for an information request

parameters.name: pageCursor

post.description:
Flags evidence for an information request when it doesn't meet audit requirements,
marking issues that need to be addressed before approval. This action changes the
request's approvalStatus to a flagged state and creates an activity log entry.

Flagging workflow:
1. Auditor reviews submitted evidence
2. If issues are found, auditor calls this endpoint with detailed reason
3. Request status changes to flagged state
4. Customer is notified and can see the reason in activity logs
5. Customer addresses issues and updates evidence
6. When ready, customer changes status back to awaiting review
7. Auditor reviews again and either flags again or accepts

The `reason` field should clearly explain what's missing or incorrect so the
customer knows exactly what to fix. This reason is visible to the customer
and appears in the activity log.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of activity logs for an information request, providing
a complete audit trail of all changes and actions.

This endpoint supports delta synchronization via the `changedSinceDate` parameter,
allowing efficient polling for changes without retrieving the entire dataset.

Pagination usage:
1. Make initial request with desired `pageSize`
2. Check `results.pageInfo.hasNextPage` to see if more data exists
3. If true, use `results.pageInfo.endCursor` as `pageCursor` in next request
4. Repeat until `hasNextPage` is false

Delta sync usage:
1. Store the timestamp of your last sync
2. Pass that timestamp as `changedSinceDate`
3. Only activity created since that timestamp is returned
4. Process updates to track all changes to the information request
5. Update your last sync timestamp to the current time

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of all evidence attached to an information request,
enabling auditors to review evidence submitted by customers.

This endpoint always includes soft-deleted records (where `deletionDate !== null`).
Clients should check the `deletionDate` field to identify and handle deleted records
appropriately in their systems.

This endpoint supports delta synchronization via the `changedSinceDate` parameter,
allowing efficient polling for changes without retrieving the entire dataset.

Pagination usage:
1. Make initial request with desired `pageSize`
2. Check `results.pageInfo.hasNextPage` to see if more data exists
3. If true, use `results.pageInfo.endCursor` as `pageCursor` in next request
4. Repeat until `hasNextPage` is false

Delta sync usage:
1. Store the timestamp of your last sync
2. Pass that timestamp as `changedSinceDate`
3. Only evidence created, modified, shared, or deleted since that timestamp is returned
4. Process updates, including soft-deletes (deletionDate !== null)
5. Update your last sync timestamp to the current time
get.summary: List evidence for an information request

get.description:
Retrieves the current status and result of a questionnaire export using the id received from either the `createQuestionnaireExport` endpoint or the `v1.questionnaire.export-completed` webhook payload.

This endpoint utilizes a dynamic response schema that changes based on the value of the status field:

  - pending: The export is currently in the queue or processing. Only base metadata is returned.
  - completed: The export finished successfully. The response expands to include completedAt and a downloadUrl. This pre-signed URL is valid for 24 hours; if it expires, simply call this endpoint again to retrieve a fresh, active link.
  - failed: The process encountered an error. The response expands to include failedAt and an errorMessage detailing the reason for failure.

Developers should first check the status string before attempting to access result-specific fields like downloadUrl or errorMessage.
